name: Introduction to the UI
description: A quick walkthrough of the main interface panels.

steps.title: The Prompt Box
steps.markdownText:
**The Prompt Box** is where you type your prompts to the AI agents.

Type your instructions here, then press **Enter** (or click the **Run** button) to send them to the active agent.

steps.title: The Run Button
steps.markdownText: The **Send**/**Queue** button dispatches your prompt to the Squad Coordinator (or queues it if agents are busy).

steps.title: The Queue Tab Strip
steps.markdownText:
**The Queue** lets you line up multiple prompts to run sequentially.

Click **+** to add a new queue item, drag items to reorder them, and use the play/pause button to control execution.

steps.title: The Help Menu
steps.markdownText: You can start guided tours from inside the **Help** menu.

name: Working with the Loop
description: Learn how the Loop panel, queue pausing, and the Health panel work together.

steps.title: The Loop Panel
steps.markdownText:
**The Loop** runs a set of prompts automatically on a schedule or file-watch trigger.

Configure your loop in `loop.md` — the Loop panel shows iteration status and lets you start, stop, or monitor progress.

steps.title: Pausing the Queue
steps.markdownText:
**Pause the queue** to temporarily hold all pending prompts.

This is useful when you want to review the current agent's work before allowing the next queued item to run.

steps.title: The Code Health Panel
steps.markdownText:
**The Code Health panel** runs configured checks against your codebase and reports results.

Use `code-health.md` to define which checks to run. Green = passing, red = action needed.
